channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Experimental realization of dice-lattice flat band at the Fermi level in layered electride YCl
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 31 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-69049-0

Using ARPES, this work identifies dice-lattice flat bands in the electride material YCl. The authors show that anionic electrons act as lattice sites to create this rare electronic structure.
item.dc:title: Experimental realization of dice-lattice flat band at the Fermi level in layered electride YCl
item.dc:creator: Songyuan Geng
Xin Wang
Risi Guo
Chen Qiu
Fangjie Chen
Qun Wang
Kangjie Li
Peipei Hao
Hanpu Liang
Yang Huang
Yunbo Wu
Shengtao Cui
Zhe Sun
Timur K. Kim
Cephise Cacho
Daniel S. Dessau
Benjamin T. Zhou
Haoxiang Li

item.title: Vertical chiral emission from an intrinsically achiral metasurface enabled with anisotropic continuum
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 31 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68728-2

Chiral light emission usually requires twisted or asymmetric nanostructures. Here, the authors show that even out-of plane symmetric metasurfaces can emit circularly polarized light, achieved by tuning an anisotropic continuum rather than breaking symmetry.
item.dc:title: Vertical chiral emission from an intrinsically achiral metasurface enabled with anisotropic continuum
item.dc:creator: Yuwei Sun
Zhipeng Hu
Mengqi Liu
Jianfeng Chen
Dmitrii Gromyko
Kezhang Shi
Lin Wu
Yi Jin
Sailing He
Cheng-Wei Qiu

item.title: Nanotwin architecture and ultra-high valley degeneracy lead to high thermoelectric performance in GeTe-based thermoelectric materials
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 31 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68908-0

Authors propose a pathway to design GeTe based thermoelectric materials with high thermoelectric performance and mechanical properties by nanotwin architecture and inducing high valley degeneracy.
item.dc:title: Nanotwin architecture and ultra-high valley degeneracy lead to high thermoelectric performance in GeTe-based thermoelectric materials
item.dc:creator: Song Li
Yuxuan Yang
Xiaoyu Fei
Yang Geng
Jiajun Nan
Pubao Peng
Guizhong Li
Yang Zhang
Xiaobing Liu
Yongsheng Zhang
Haijun Wu
Guodong Tang

item.title: Modulating thermo-diffusion/galvanic coupling via ion speciation engineering enables high-performance ionic thermoelectric cells
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 31 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-68721-9

Coupling two ionic thermoelectric effects in n-type materials is scarce, restricting the development of high-performance systems. Here, the authors present an ionic-thermoelectric material with interactive thermo-diffusion/galvanic coupling effect based on coordination chemistry.
item.dc:title: Modulating thermo-diffusion/galvanic coupling via ion speciation engineering enables high-performance ionic thermoelectric cells
item.dc:creator: Yuchen Li
Ying-Ru Qiu
Jinsha Liao
Helios Y. Li
Zhouyuntong Liu
Yuxiang Chen
Mao Yu
Lizhi Xu
Chang Liu
Jefferson Zhe Liu
Jian-Feng Li
Weishu Liu
Yao-Hui Wang
Nicholas X. Fang
